catalysts.thesis: Aggressive insider buying immediately following a severe earnings-driven selloff points to deep mispricing and an impending bottom.
catalysts.catalyst_details: Following a 27.9% stock crash on March 4, 2026, due to an earnings miss, multiple insiders stepped into the open market to purchase millions of dollars in shares through mid-to-late March, signaling extreme conviction despite active litigation.
catalysts.absorption_status: The massive insider accumulation is completely unpriced, as the market remains fixated on the 'kitchen sink' Q4 earnings and subsequent class-action lawsuits [1.6].
catalysts.risk: Active securities fraud class-action lawsuits and fundamental margin pressure could drag down retail sentiment.
catalysts.mispricing_evidence: Coordinated insider buying of this magnitude directly contradicts the market's ongoing panic selloff, indicating that the bad news is already heavily priced in.

catalysts.thesis: A massive $12.6M block of insider buying near 52-week lows creates a highly favorable risk/reward setup supported by a double-digit dividend yield.
catalysts.catalyst_details: Insider Steven B. Klinsky and others executed over $12.6 million in open-market buy transactions throughout March 2026, without a single sell transaction recorded, representing a massive bet on the company's underlying asset value.
catalysts.absorption_status: The stock has trended down by 8.6% over the last 90 days, sitting near 52-week lows ($7.51), meaning the market has completely ignored the $12.6M insider bid.
catalysts.risk: Macro interest rate exposure which typically affects Business Development Company (BDC) net asset values and investment income.
catalysts.mispricing_evidence: The stock's continued drift lower completely diverges from the exceptionally high-conviction, multi-million dollar insider block purchases.

catalysts.thesis: A solid Q4 EPS beat amid depressed year-to-date market sentiment sets up a classic Post-Earnings Announcement Drift (PEAD) opportunity.
catalysts.catalyst_details: On March 24, 2026, Core & Main reported Q4 earnings of $0.52 per share, cleanly beating the $0.48 consensus estimate (+8.33% surprise). Shares remain down nearly 7% YTD, providing a compelling valuation entry.
catalysts.absorption_status: The stock has yet to materially gap up or sustain momentum from the earnings beat on the day of the release, keeping it well below the 8% exclusion rule.
catalysts.risk: Slight top-line revenue miss ($1.58B actual vs $1.59B expected) and broader infrastructure spending delays.
catalysts.expected_upside: 12-18% upward drift as analysts revise estimates
catalysts.mispricing_evidence: The +8.33% earnings surprise demonstrates operational efficiency, yet the stock has underperformed the S&P 500 YTD, leaving room for an upward re-rating.
